sections.title: Key Risks
sections.content:
• Tariffs and trade measures could materially impact supply chain, costs, and gross margins. (Evidence: Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced... Tariffs and other measures that are applied to the Company’s products or their components can have a material adverse impact)
• Foreign exchange rate fluctuations may adversely affect net sales and gross margins. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to foreign exchange rate risk relates primarily to the Company being a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in exchange rates, and in particular a stre)
• Legal proceedings, including EU DMA investigations and DOJ antitrust lawsuit, could result in significant fines or operational changes. (Evidence: On April 23, 2025, the Commission fined the Company €500 million in the Article 5(4) Investigation... The DOJ is seeking equitable relief to redress the alleged anticompetitive behavior.)
• Credit risk on trade receivables and vendor non-trade receivables is heightened in adverse economic conditions. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to credit and collectibility risk on its trade receivables is higher in certain international markets... As of September 27, 2025, the Company’s vendor non-trade receivables wer)
• Changes in tax laws, including global minimum tax standards, could increase effective tax rate. (Evidence: The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development continues to advance proposals for modernizing international tax rules, including the introduction of global minimum tax standards.)

sections.title: Financial Overview
sections.content:
• Total Net Sales: $416,161M (vs. $391,035M) — +6.4% — Growth driven by Services (+14%) and iPhone (+4%).
• Net Income: $112,010M (vs. $93,736M) — +19.5% — Benefited from lower effective tax rate (15.6% vs. 24.1%).
• Diluted EPS: $7.46 (vs. $6.08) — +22.7% — Reflects net income growth and share repurchases.
• Gross Margin: 46.9% (vs. 46.2%) — +0.7 pts — Services gross margin improved to 75.4% from 73.9%.
• Operating Cash Flow: $111,482M (vs. $118,254M) — -5.7% — Decrease due to changes in working capital.
• Total Assets: $359,241M (vs. $364,980M) — -1.6% — Decline driven by lower marketable securities.
• Cash & Equivalents: $35,934M (vs. $29,943M) — +20.0% — Increase reflects cash generation offset by capital returns.
• Total Debt: $98,657M (vs. $106,629M) — -7.5% — Includes $12.4B current term debt and $8.0B commercial paper.

sections.title: Management Commentary
sections.content:
Themes: - Services growth remains a key driver, with advertising, App Store, and cloud services contributing to a 14% increase.
- iPhone sales growth was led by Pro models, while Mac and iPad also saw increases.
- Tariffs began impacting Products gross margin in 2025, with management expecting continued volatility and downward pressure on gross margins.
- R&D spending continues to rise, focusing on headcount and infrastructure to support product innovation.
Quotes: - Speaker: Management
Quote: Products gross margin increased during 2025 compared to 2024 primarily due to favorable costs and a different mix of products, partially offset by tariff costs.
Context: MD&A discussion of gross margin drivers.
- Speaker: Management
Quote: The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.
Context: Forward-looking statement on margin trends.
Capital Allocation: - Repurchased $89.3B of common stock and paid $15.4B in dividends in FY2025.
- Announced a new $100B share repurchase program in May 2025, with $221M utilized as of September 27, 2025.
- Quarterly dividend raised to $0.26 per share from $0.25.
- Capital expenditures were $12.7B, up from $9.4B in 2024.
Source Section Ref: Item 7. MD&A

sections.title: Strategic Developments
sections.content:
Guidance: Not disclosed—no formal guidance provided in the excerpts.
Tone: cautious
Drivers: - Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, mix, and other factors.
- Services growth expected to continue, but macroeconomic conditions and currency fluctuations remain risks.
Watch Items: - Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.
- Outcome of EU DMA appeals and DOJ antitrust lawsuit.
- Consumer spending trends amid macroeconomic uncertainty.
Source Section Ref: Item 7. MD&A
Forward Guidance: Not disclosed—no formal guidance provided in the excerpts.

summary.business_overview:
*Auto-generated from structured data because the writer output failed validation (Editorial summary length must be 100-320 words (current: 414).).*
## Executive Summary
Apple reports FY2025 revenue of $416.2B (+6% YoY), net income of $112.0B, and diluted EPS of $7.46, driven by Services growth and iPhone gains, while navigating tariff impacts and legal challenges. Total net sales increased 6% to $416.2B, with Services up 14% to $109.2B and Products up 4% to $307.0B.; Net income rose 19% to $112.0B, aided by a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in 2024, primarily due to a $10.7B decrease in provision related to the State Aid Decision.; Gross margin expanded 70 bps to 46.9%, with Services gross margin reaching 75.4%.; The company returned $104.7B to shareholders through $89.3B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends, and announced a new $100B buyback program.; Legal and regulatory risks include a €500M EU fine under the DMA, an ongoing DOJ antitrust lawsuit, and an injunction in the Epic Games case.

## Financials
- Total Net Sales: $416,161M vs. $391,035M (+6.4%) – Growth driven by Services (+14%) and iPhone (+4%).
- Net Income: $112,010M vs. $93,736M (+19.5%) – Benefited from lower effective tax rate (15.6% vs. 24.1%).
- Diluted EPS: $7.46 vs. $6.08 (+22.7%) – Reflects net income growth and share repurchases.
- Gross Margin: 46.9% vs. 46.2% (+0.7 pts) – Services gross margin improved to 75.4% from 73.9%.
- Operating Cash Flow: $111,482M vs. $118,254M (-5.7%) – Decrease due to changes in working capital.
- Total Assets: $359,241M vs. $364,980M (-1.6%) – Decline driven by lower marketable securities.
- Cash & Equivalents: $35,934M vs. $29,943M (+20.0%) – Increase reflects cash generation offset by capital returns.
- Total Debt: $98,657M vs. $106,629M (-7.5%) – Includes $12.4B current term debt and $8.0B commercial paper.
- Profitability: Products gross margin increased to $112.9B (+3.0%) but margin percentage dipped to 36.8% from 37.2% due to mix and tariff costs.; Services gross margin surged to $82.3B (+15.9%) with margin percentage up 150 bps to 75.4%.; Operating expenses rose 8% to $62.2B, with R&D up 10% to $34.6B and SG&A up 6% to $27.6B, both driven by headcount and infrastructure costs.
- Cash flow: Operating cash flow of $111.5B was down $6.8B YoY, primarily due to changes in other current and non-current liabilities.; Free cash flow (operating CF minus capex) was $98.8B, compared to $108.8B in 2024.; Investing activities generated $15.2B, driven by net maturities of marketable securities.
- Balance sheet: Total assets of $359.2B, with $132.4B in cash, equivalents, and marketable securities.; Total debt of $98.7B, with $12.4B current term debt and $8.0B commercial paper.; Shareholders' equity increased to $73.7B from $57.0B, despite $90.1B in repurchases, due to net income and share-based compensation.

## Risks
- Tariffs and trade measures could materially impact supply chain, costs, and gross margins. (Evidence: Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced... Tariffs and other measures that are applied to the Company’s products or their components can have a material adverse impact on the Company’s business, results of operations and financial condition...)
- Foreign exchange rate fluctuations may adversely affect net sales and gross margins. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to foreign exchange rate risk relates primarily to the Company being a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in exchange rates, and in particular a strengthening of the U.S. dollar, will negatively affect the Company’s net sales and gross margins as expressed in U.S. dollars.)
- Legal proceedings, including EU DMA investigations and DOJ antitrust lawsuit, could result in significant fines or operational changes. (Evidence: On April 23, 2025, the Commission fined the Company €500 million in the Article 5(4) Investigation... The DOJ is seeking equitable relief to redress the alleged anticompetitive behavior.)
- Credit risk on trade receivables and vendor non-trade receivables is heightened in adverse economic conditions. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to credit and collectibility risk on its trade receivables is higher in certain international markets... As of September 27, 2025, the Company’s vendor non-trade receivables were concentrated among a few individual vendors located primarily in Asia.)
- Changes in tax laws, including global minimum tax standards, could increase effective tax rate. (Evidence: The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development continues to advance proposals for modernizing international tax rules, including the introduction of global minimum tax standards.)

## Management Commentary
- Themes: Services growth remains a key driver, with advertising, App Store, and cloud services contributing to a 14% increase.; iPhone sales growth was led by Pro models, while Mac and iPad also saw increases.; Tariffs began impacting Products gross margin in 2025, with management expecting continued volatility and downward pressure on gross margins.; R&D spending continues to rise, focusing on headcount and infrastructure to support product innovation.
- Capital allocation: Repurchased $89.3B of common stock and paid $15.4B in dividends in FY2025.; Announced a new $100B share repurchase program in May 2025, with $221M utilized as of September 27, 2025.; Quarterly dividend raised to $0.26 per share from $0.25.; Capital expenditures were $12.7B, up from $9.4B in 2024.
> "Products gross margin increased during 2025 compared to 2024 primarily due to favorable costs and a different mix of products, partially offset by tariff costs." – Management
> "The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure." – Management

## Outlook
- Guidance: Not disclosed—no formal guidance provided in the excerpts.
- Tone: cautious
- Drivers: Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, mix, and other factors.; Services growth expected to continue, but macroeconomic conditions and currency fluctuations remain risks.
- Watch items: Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.; Outcome of EU DMA appeals and DOJ antitrust lawsuit.; Consumer spending trends amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

segment_performance.commentary: Higher iPhone and Services sales, partially offset by unfavorable currency impact.
Growth driven by Services, iPhone, and Mac.
Lower iPhone sales partially offset by higher Mac sales.
Higher sales of iPhone, Services, and iPad.
Growth from iPhone, Services, and Mac.

liquidity_capital_structure.leverage: Total debt of $98.7B, with a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.34, down from 1.87 in 2024 due to increased equity.
liquidity_capital_structure.liquidity: Cash, equivalents, and marketable securities of $132.4B, with $35.9B in cash and equivalents. Management believes this is sufficient to meet cash requirements and capital return program for the next 12 months and beyond.
shareholder_returns: Repurchased $89.3B of common stock in FY2025, with $90.1B total including net share settlement.
Paid $15.4B in dividends and dividend equivalents.
New $100B share repurchase program authorized in May 2025.
liquidity_capital_structure.source_section_ref: Item 7. MD&A, Liquidity and Capital Resources

debt_covenants: Not disclosed—no specific debt covenant details provided in the excerpts.
contingent_liabilities: EU DMA investigation: €500M fine imposed in April 2025, under appeal; additional potential fines up to 10% of annual worldwide net sales.
DOJ antitrust lawsuit: seeking equitable relief for alleged monopolization; various civil lawsuits also pending.
Epic Games: injunction prohibiting commissions on out-of-app purchases; appeal pending, with expedited hearing in October 2025.
Deemed repatriation tax payable: $8.8B due within 12 months.

guidance_outlook.guidance: Not disclosed—no formal guidance provided in the excerpts.

Consumer spending trends amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

notable_footnotes.impact: Will modernize accounting for internal-use software, effective Q1 2029; early adoption permitted.

notable_footnotes.item: State Aid Decision impact on tax provision
notable_footnotes.impact: Provision for income taxes decreased by $10.7B YoY due to the State Aid Decision, contributing to lower effective tax rate.
notable_footnotes.source_section_ref: Item 7. MD&A, Provision for Income Taxes

three_year_trend.trend_summary: Revenue grew from $383.3B in 2023 to $416.2B in 2025, a 3-year CAGR of 4.2%. Net income increased from $97.0B to $112.0B, with a dip in 2024 due to a higher tax provision. Services revenue nearly doubled its contribution from $85.2B to $109.2B, now 26% of total sales. Gross margin expanded from 44.1% to 46.9%, driven by Services mix shift. Operating cash flow remained strong but declined slightly in 2025. Capital returns exceeded $100B annually, funded by cash generation and debt.
inflections: 2024 net income declined to $93.7B from $97.0B in 2023 due to a $29.7B tax provision (including State Aid Decision impact), before rebounding to $112.0B in 2025.
Services gross margin crossed 75% in 2025, up from 70.8% in 2023, reflecting scale and mix benefits.
Greater China revenue declined 4% in 2025 after an 8% drop in 2024, indicating ongoing challenges in the region.

insights: Revenue growth accelerated from 2% in 2024 to 6% in 2025, driven by Services and iPhone.
Effective tax rate swung from 14.7% (2023) to 24.1% (2024) to 15.6% (2025), heavily influenced by one-time tax items.
Share repurchases remained elevated at $89-96B annually, reducing diluted share count by ~2.6% per year.

quotes.quote: The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.

capital_allocation: Repurchased $89.3B of common stock and paid $15.4B in dividends in FY2025.
Announced a new $100B share repurchase program in May 2025, with $221M utilized as of September 27, 2025.
Quarterly dividend raised to $0.26 per share from $0.25.
Capital expenditures were $12.7B, up from $9.4B in 2024.

segment_performance.commentary: Higher iPhone and Services sales, partially offset by unfavorable currency impact.
Growth driven by Services, iPhone, and Mac.
Lower iPhone sales partially offset by higher Mac sales.
Higher sales of iPhone, Services, and iPad.
Growth from iPhone, Services, and Mac.

liquidity_capital_structure.leverage: Total debt of $98.7B, with a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.34, down from 1.87 in 2024 due to increased equity.
liquidity_capital_structure.liquidity: Cash, equivalents, and marketable securities of $132.4B, with $35.9B in cash and equivalents. Management believes this is sufficient to meet cash requirements and capital return program for the next 12 months and beyond.
shareholder_returns: Repurchased $89.3B of common stock in FY2025, with $90.1B total including net share settlement.
Paid $15.4B in dividends and dividend equivalents.
New $100B share repurchase program authorized in May 2025.
liquidity_capital_structure.source_section_ref: Item 7. MD&A, Liquidity and Capital Resources

debt_covenants: Not disclosed—no specific debt covenant details provided in the excerpts.
contingent_liabilities: EU DMA investigation: €500M fine imposed in April 2025, under appeal; additional potential fines up to 10% of annual worldwide net sales.
DOJ antitrust lawsuit: seeking equitable relief for alleged monopolization; various civil lawsuits also pending.
Epic Games: injunction prohibiting commissions on out-of-app purchases; appeal pending, with expedited hearing in October 2025.
Deemed repatriation tax payable: $8.8B due within 12 months.

guidance_outlook.guidance: Not disclosed—no formal guidance provided in the excerpts.
drivers: Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, mix, and other factors.
Services growth expected to continue, but macroeconomic conditions and currency fluctuations remain risks.
watch_items: Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.
Outcome of EU DMA appeals and DOJ antitrust lawsuit.
Consumer spending trends amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

notable_footnotes.impact: Enhanced segment disclosures, including significant expenses regularly provided to the CODM.

notable_footnotes.impact: Will modernize accounting for internal-use software, effective Q1 2029; early adoption permitted.

notable_footnotes.item: State Aid Decision impact on tax provision
notable_footnotes.impact: Provision for income taxes decreased by $10.7B YoY due to the State Aid Decision, contributing to lower effective tax rate.
notable_footnotes.source_section_ref: Item 7. MD&A, Provision for Income Taxes

three_year_trend.trend_summary: Revenue grew from $383.3B in 2023 to $416.2B in 2025, a 3-year CAGR of 4.2%. Net income increased from $97.0B to $112.0B, with a dip in 2024 due to a higher tax provision. Services revenue nearly doubled its contribution from $85.2B to $109.2B, now 26% of total sales. Gross margin expanded from 44.1% to 46.9%, driven by Services mix shift. Operating cash flow remained strong but declined slightly in 2025. Capital returns exceeded $100B annually, funded by cash generation and debt.
inflections: 2024 net income declined to $93.7B from $97.0B in 2023 due to a $29.7B tax provision (including State Aid Decision impact), before rebounding to $112.0B in 2025.
Services gross margin crossed 75% in 2025, up from 70.8% in 2023, reflecting scale and mix benefits.
Greater China revenue declined 4% in 2025 after an 8% drop in 2024, indicating ongoing challenges in the region.

insights: Revenue growth accelerated from 2% in 2024 to 6% in 2025, driven by Services and iPhone.
Effective tax rate swung from 14.7% (2023) to 24.1% (2024) to 15.6% (2025), heavily influenced by one-time tax items.
Share repurchases remained elevated at $89-96B annually, reducing diluted share count by ~2.6% per year.

writer.markdown:
*Auto-generated from structured data because the writer output failed validation (Editorial summary length must be 100-320 words (current: 414).).*
## Executive Summary
Apple reports FY2025 revenue of $416.2B (+6% YoY), net income of $112.0B, and diluted EPS of $7.46, driven by Services growth and iPhone gains, while navigating tariff impacts and legal challenges. Total net sales increased 6% to $416.2B, with Services up 14% to $109.2B and Products up 4% to $307.0B.; Net income rose 19% to $112.0B, aided by a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in 2024, primarily due to a $10.7B decrease in provision related to the State Aid Decision.; Gross margin expanded 70 bps to 46.9%, with Services gross margin reaching 75.4%.; The company returned $104.7B to shareholders through $89.3B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends, and announced a new $100B buyback program.; Legal and regulatory risks include a €500M EU fine under the DMA, an ongoing DOJ antitrust lawsuit, and an injunction in the Epic Games case.

## Financials
- Total Net Sales: $416,161M vs. $391,035M (+6.4%) – Growth driven by Services (+14%) and iPhone (+4%).
- Net Income: $112,010M vs. $93,736M (+19.5%) – Benefited from lower effective tax rate (15.6% vs. 24.1%).
- Diluted EPS: $7.46 vs. $6.08 (+22.7%) – Reflects net income growth and share repurchases.
- Gross Margin: 46.9% vs. 46.2% (+0.7 pts) – Services gross margin improved to 75.4% from 73.9%.
- Operating Cash Flow: $111,482M vs. $118,254M (-5.7%) – Decrease due to changes in working capital.
- Total Assets: $359,241M vs. $364,980M (-1.6%) – Decline driven by lower marketable securities.
- Cash & Equivalents: $35,934M vs. $29,943M (+20.0%) – Increase reflects cash generation offset by capital returns.
- Total Debt: $98,657M vs. $106,629M (-7.5%) – Includes $12.4B current term debt and $8.0B commercial paper.
- Profitability: Products gross margin increased to $112.9B (+3.0%) but margin percentage dipped to 36.8% from 37.2% due to mix and tariff costs.; Services gross margin surged to $82.3B (+15.9%) with margin percentage up 150 bps to 75.4%.; Operating expenses rose 8% to $62.2B, with R&D up 10% to $34.6B and SG&A up 6% to $27.6B, both driven by headcount and infrastructure costs.
- Cash flow: Operating cash flow of $111.5B was down $6.8B YoY, primarily due to changes in other current and non-current liabilities.; Free cash flow (operating CF minus capex) was $98.8B, compared to $108.8B in 2024.; Investing activities generated $15.2B, driven by net maturities of marketable securities.
- Balance sheet: Total assets of $359.2B, with $132.4B in cash, equivalents, and marketable securities.; Total debt of $98.7B, with $12.4B current term debt and $8.0B commercial paper.; Shareholders' equity increased to $73.7B from $57.0B, despite $90.1B in repurchases, due to net income and share-based compensation.

## Risks
- Tariffs and trade measures could materially impact supply chain, costs, and gross margins. (Evidence: Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced... Tariffs and other measures that are applied to the Company’s products or their components can have a material adverse impact on the Company’s business, results of operations and financial condition...)
- Foreign exchange rate fluctuations may adversely affect net sales and gross margins. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to foreign exchange rate risk relates primarily to the Company being a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in exchange rates, and in particular a strengthening of the U.S. dollar, will negatively affect the Company’s net sales and gross margins as expressed in U.S. dollars.)
- Legal proceedings, including EU DMA investigations and DOJ antitrust lawsuit, could result in significant fines or operational changes. (Evidence: On April 23, 2025, the Commission fined the Company €500 million in the Article 5(4) Investigation... The DOJ is seeking equitable relief to redress the alleged anticompetitive behavior.)
- Credit risk on trade receivables and vendor non-trade receivables is heightened in adverse economic conditions. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to credit and collectibility risk on its trade receivables is higher in certain international markets... As of September 27, 2025, the Company’s vendor non-trade receivables were concentrated among a few individual vendors located primarily in Asia.)
- Changes in tax laws, including global minimum tax standards, could increase effective tax rate. (Evidence: The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development continues to advance proposals for modernizing international tax rules, including the introduction of global minimum tax standards.)

## Management Commentary
- Themes: Services growth remains a key driver, with advertising, App Store, and cloud services contributing to a 14% increase.; iPhone sales growth was led by Pro models, while Mac and iPad also saw increases.; Tariffs began impacting Products gross margin in 2025, with management expecting continued volatility and downward pressure on gross margins.; R&D spending continues to rise, focusing on headcount and infrastructure to support product innovation.
- Capital allocation: Repurchased $89.3B of common stock and paid $15.4B in dividends in FY2025.; Announced a new $100B share repurchase program in May 2025, with $221M utilized as of September 27, 2025.; Quarterly dividend raised to $0.26 per share from $0.25.; Capital expenditures were $12.7B, up from $9.4B in 2024.
> "Products gross margin increased during 2025 compared to 2024 primarily due to favorable costs and a different mix of products, partially offset by tariff costs." – Management
> "The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure." – Management

## Outlook
- Guidance: Not disclosed—no formal guidance provided in the excerpts.
- Tone: cautious
- Drivers: Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, mix, and other factors.; Services growth expected to continue, but macroeconomic conditions and currency fluctuations remain risks.
- Watch items: Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.; Outcome of EU DMA appeals and DOJ antitrust lawsuit.; Consumer spending trends amid macroeconomic uncertainty.
